business_question: Which patient age groups generate the highest claim denial rates over the last 2 years?
business_context: Revenue cycle leaders want to understand whether certain patient populations are more prone to claim denials.

aggregation_logic.Denied Claims: COUNT(CASE WHEN is_denied=TRUE THEN claim_id END)

sql_answer:
SELECT
    p.age_group,
    COUNT(c.claim_id) AS total_claims,
    SUM(CASE WHEN c.is_denied = TRUE THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS denied_claims,
    ROUND(
        SUM(CASE WHEN c.is_denied = TRUE THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0
        / COUNT(c.claim_id), 2
    ) AS denial_rate_pct
FROM fact_claims c
JOIN dim_patient p
    ON c.patient_id = p.patient_id
WHERE c.submitted_date >= DATEADD(YEAR, -2, CURRENT_DATE)
GROUP BY p.age_group
ORDER BY denial_rate_pct DESC;
